title: Final Oscar Predictions: Best Supporting Actress — Will Amy Madigan Become the Second Oldest Winner, or Will Wunmi Mosaku or Teyana Taylor Make History?
text: Amy Madigan (Weapons) holds the leading position, having won critics' awards including Critics Choice and SAG Awards. Teyana Taylor (One Battle After Another) represents a formidable challenger, having uniquely swept all four major televised precursor awards, including a Golden Globe win. Wunmi Mosaku (Sinners) claimed the BAFTA award and comes from a best picture favorite. Should Taylor or Mosaku prevail, they would mark the third consecutive Best Supporting Actress win for Black women, following Da'Vine Joy Randolph and Zoe Saldaña—a first in the Academy's 98-year history. If Madigan wins at 75, she would become the second-oldest winner in this category, trailing only Peggy Ashcroft (77).
